Open infrastructure for the energy economy

The next energy revolution is not about generating more energy. It's about coordinating it.

Homes are becoming power producers. EVs are becoming batteries on wheels. Digital Energy Grid gives every one of them a shared way to coordinate, transact and grow the energy economy together.

Build with DEG → See DEG in action
Energy value, finally free to move.
The problem

The challenge is no longer producing more energy. It is enabling millions of participants to work together.

Every interaction
sharing data verifying assets discovering opportunities exchanging value enforcing policy

— stitched through bespoke integrations, fragmented platforms and bilateral agreements.

As more participants join, the system gets more complex, more brittle, more expensive to scale.

What DEG is

The universal shared layer that coordinates the flows.

Open, interoperable infrastructure that lets every participant discover one another, exchange trusted data, and transact over common rails.

Utilities stay utilities. Marketplaces stay marketplaces. Everyone just stops rebuilding the plumbing between them. Coordination becomes infrastructure.

What becomes possible

When coordination becomes infrastructure, entirely new markets emerge.

Built once. Available everywhere.

  1. 01

    Invisible surplus generation turns into a verifiable income stream.

  2. 02

    Electric vehicles can join flexibility markets — regardless of who made them or who runs the charger.

  3. 03

    A microgrid's verified performance becomes a financeable asset.

  4. 04

    Demand-response events become the data point that lowers insurance premiums.

  5. 05

    A prosumer's generation record becomes an income credential a lender can read.

Eight building blocks · infinite innovation

Eight programmable building blocks. Countless applications.

Reusable foundations to build on, instead of rebuilding them for every application. Interoperability from day one.

01

Identity & addressability

02

Federated registries & trust

03

Consent & data exchange

04

Standardised data models

05

Common transport protocols

06

Energy contracts

07

Policy as code

08

Observability & decisioning

The bigger picture

Every major transformation begins with shared infrastructure.

then
Roads

enabled commerce to move.

industrial
Electric grids

enabled industrialisation.

now
Digital Energy Grid

enables an open, AI-native energy economy.

The next energy markets will run on open infrastructure — where every participant, not just large institutions, can transact with trust.
